links.targetTitle: A cross-user share is three Swarm objects: public profile, share manifest, and ACT-protected share bundle

global.title: Phase 2b added raw-file sharing in chat with ACT-encrypted /bzz uploads up to 200MB.
global.content:
## Scope

Phase 2b was not in the original design; it was added during implementation to cover raw-file sharing alongside document sharing.

## What shipped

- **ACT-encrypted upload** via `/bzz` with a grantee list of both parties' Bee pubkeys
- `FileAttachment` type with inline preview cards per mime type (images, PDFs, audio, video, text)
- **Drag-and-drop zone** in the chat thread
- **Files tab** with thumbnail grid and a universal preview modal
- Click-to-load video with pre-flight `canPlayType` check and download fallback when the browser can't play the codec
- External `bzz://<hash>` link previews routed through the same render path (no ACT, public content, badged as such)
- 200 MB file cap enforced on both client and adapter, with broad mime-guess coverage

## Design parity

File sharing and document sharing use the same ACT envelope and the same grantee shape — any difference between them lives above ACT, in how the attachment card renders. This keeps the Bee-node surface identical for both flows.
global.description: Beyond document sharing, chat supports raw files (images, PDFs, audio, video, text) via ACT-encrypted /bzz uploads with both parties as grantees, capped at 200MB.
